exemplar.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a sustained and clear focus on the prescribed title with a wellâanchored definition of âwonderâ
Effectively links both Natural Sciences and Human Sciences with well-chosen, concrete examples
Arguments are generally coherent, logically sequenced and supported by specific illustrations
Demonstrates awareness of contrasting perspectives and reflects on broader implications in the conclusion
weaknesses: Evaluation of counter-arguments and alternative points of view remains surface-level and would benefit from deeper probing
Some examples are treated as illustrative anecdotes rather than critically analysed evidence
Occasional generalizations and descriptive passages dilute analytical rigor
Underlying assumptions and limitations of key claims are not consistently examined
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examiner has awarded a total of 8 marks, and although the strand assessment described the work as bordering the 7-8 band (leaning toward the lower end), the overall qualityâespecially the clear focus, coherent argumentation and effective use of examplesâwarrants full alignment with the 7-8 descriptor. In reverse grading mode, we align the single gradable criterion with the final total of 8 while recognizing that the essay met the key requirements of the 7-8 band. The student showed sufficient depth, coherence and engagement with multiple perspectives to justify the top mark within this band, bringing the total to the required 8.

annotations.comment: The introduction sets up the essayâs scope well by previewing AOKs; to strengthen focus, the student should articulate a clear knowledge question in TOK format to guide analysis throughout.

The Newton versus Einstein narrative demonstrates tension between accepted frameworks and innovation; yet, the student could enhance evaluation by exploring why communities resisted relativity and its implications for wonder.
Strength: The student identifies a clear example of paradigm shift in the Natural Sciences, showing awareness of historical resistance to new theories.
This exposition of Goodallâs findings is clear; to enrich coherence, explicitly tie the emergence of new questions on emotion and communication back to the essayâs title about wonder.
The Andalusia and Bavaria examples are illustrative; to deepen analysis, examine structural factors (e.g., policy, culture) that mediate over-specializationâs impact on collective wonder.
Strength: The student employs concrete, contrasting real-world examples which ground theoretical discussion in tangible contexts.
The integration of economic theory concepts (absolute & comparative advantage) is precise; to link with TOK analysis, evaluate how these frameworks influence economistsâ sense of wonder.
Strength: The conclusion brings the essay full circle by raising further questions, showing intellectual curiosity consistent with the theme of wonder.
The student successfully reflects on implications for society; to deepen, they could tie these reflections back to specific AOK examples to reinforce coherence.
Strength: The introduction clearly defines âwonderâ in TOK terms, providing a solid conceptual foundation for subsequent analysis.
The citation â(Liberto)â is inserted abruptly; the student should integrate references more fluidly and critically evaluate source reliability within the analysis.

The behavioural economics example illustrates how prior knowledge can spark curiosity; however, the link to âwonderâ remains implicit. The student should explicitly connect insights to the TOK concept of wonder.

The student defines âwonderâ succinctly; however, the definition could be enriched by linking it explicitly to a knowledge question, clarifying how curiosity and innovation function as TOK concepts.
Strength: The preview of both AOKs in the introduction provides clarity on the essayâs structure and scope.

The opening paragraph provides useful context on methods of knowledge acquisition; nonetheless, it could be tightened by removing generalizations and integrating these distinctions directly with the thesis on wonder.
The student outlines multiple methods of knowledge production; to strengthen analytical rigor, they should link these methods explicitly to how they nurture or constrain wonder in each AOK.
Strength: The paragraph demonstrates clear understanding of interdisciplinary methods in the Human Sciences, effectively contextualizing behavioural economics.

The explanation of behavioural economics serves as relevant evidence; to deepen analysis, the student should interrogate underlying assumptions of interdisciplinary knowledge and its direct impact on wonder.

The summary of Einsteinâs theory provides good historical detail, but the claim that he did not receive a Nobel Prize for relativity oversimplifies Nobel committee criteria. The student should refine accuracy with scholarly sources.
The rhetorical claim about inevitability in Newtonian physics is compelling; to demonstrate critical thinking, the student should assess counterarguments that scientists did anticipate anomalies.

The paragraph on over-reliance cogently argues stifled wonder; to broaden perspective, consider counter-examples where established frameworks still fostered curiosity, highlighting nuanced implications.
The paragraph effectively uses strong language (e.g., 'clutch onto established frameworks'); to maintain academic tone, consider moderating emotive phrasing.
The student refers to broad real-world applications of behavioural economics; evaluating specific cases and their effect on wonder would enhance analytical depth.
Strength: The student effectively traces the emergence of behavioural economics, demonstrating how interdisciplinary approaches can drive innovation.
Consider examining how Einsteinâs revolution changed scientistsâ curiosity about the unknown, rather than focusing solely on resistance, to more fully explore wonder.
Strength: Using Jane Goodall as an example effectively illustrates how new empirical evidence can expand wonder through paradigm challenges and social implications.

Jane Goodallâs research is a strong illustration of challenging paradigms; yet the student could bolster this by evaluating methodological limitations and reflecting on how these affect broader wonder.
The mention of women inspired into STEM is valuable but peripheral; consider reflecting on how social wonder differs from scientific wonder to sharpen focus.
Strength: The topic shift to specialisation is smoothly integrated, demonstrating coherence in linking Human Sciences examples with the essayâs theme.
The introduction of specialization lacks reference to Human Sciences methods; specifying how empirical or theoretical approaches shape specialization would clarify argument.
The conclusion raises interesting further questions; it could be strengthened by succinctly evaluating the implications of the essayâs main arguments and acknowledging its own limitations.

described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
